Title: Add content item
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Items
2. Click 'Add'
3. Fill in content item details (name, type, HTML)
4. Click 'Create'

Title: Edit content item (name/description/type/HTML)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content item exists [MKT-001]
Steps:
1. Open content item > Update name > Save
2. Update description > Save
3. Add new content type > Save
4. Update HTML > Save

Title: Delete content item and bulk delete
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content items exist
Steps:
1. Delete single content item > Confirm
2. Select multiple items > Bulk delete > Confirm

Title: Search content items
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple content items exist
Steps:
1. Enter search term in list view
2. Verify matching results

Title: Add/edit/delete content folder
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Add content folder > Fill name and description > Create
2. Edit folder name > Save
3. Edit folder description > Save
4. Delete folder

Title: Add placeholder
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Placeholders
2. Click 'Add'
3. Fill in placeholder details
4. Click 'Create'

Title: Add/update image to placeholder (drag&drop and upload)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Placeholder exists [MKT-006]
Steps:
1. Add image via drag&drop > Verify
2. Add image via upload file > Verify
3. Update image via drag&drop > Verify
4. Update image via upload > Verify

Title: Edit/delete placeholder and search
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Placeholders exist
Steps:
1. Edit placeholder name > Save
2. Edit placeholder description > Save
3. Delete via right-click context menu
4. Delete via bulk delete
5. Search placeholders in list

Title: Add/edit/delete placeholder folder
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Add placeholder folder > Fill name and description > Create
2. Edit folder name > Save
3. Edit folder description > Save
4. Delete folder

Title: Add content publishing
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Publishing
2. Click 'Add'
3. Fill in publishing details
4. Click 'Create'

Title: Edit content publishing (name/description/store/dates)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Edit name > Save
2. Edit description > Save
3. Edit store > Save
4. Edit start date > Save
5. Edit end date > Save

Title: Add placeholder and content item to publishing
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Placeholder and content item exist
Steps:
1. Add placeholder to content publishing
2. Add content item to content publishing
3. Save

Title: Content publishing Is Active toggle and search
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Set Is Active = FALSE > Save > Verify on storefront
2. Set Is Active = TRUE > Save > Verify on storefront
3. Search in list view

Title: Delete content publishing
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishings exist
Steps:
1. Delete via bulk delete > Confirm
2. Delete on details blade > Confirm

Title: Publishing conditions (add/edit/delete and apply types)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists
Steps:
1. Add publishing condition > Configure > Save
2. Edit condition > Save
3. Verify type of applying: 'all' (all conditions must match)
4. Verify type of applying: 'any' (any condition can match)
5. Delete condition

Title: Geo location and browse behavior conditions
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing with conditions
Steps:
1. Set condition: Browsing from timezone offset > Verify
2. Set condition: Browsing from zip/postal code > Verify
3. Set condition: Browsing from city/country/state > Verify
4. Set condition: Shopper searched for phrase > Verify
5. Set condition: Current language > Verify

Title: Shopper profile and catalog conditions
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing with conditions
Steps:
1. Set condition: Shopper age > Verify
2. Set condition: Shopper gender > Verify
3. Set condition: User groups > Verify
4. Set condition: Category > Verify
5. Set condition: Product > Verify

Title: Visitor targeting (everyone/registered/first-time/user groups)
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Promotion exists
Steps:
1. Set target: Everyone > Verify all users see promotion
2. Set target: Registered user > Verify
3. Set target: First time buyer > Verify
4. Set target: User groups > Verify

Title: Publishing Condition — All Stores
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Navigate to Publishing Conditions section
4. Click Add condition
5. Select condition type "Store" and set value to All Stores
6. Save the publishing
7. Verify the condition is saved and displayed
8. Check that content appears on all configured stores in storefront

Title: Publishing Condition — Specific Store
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Multiple stores configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Navigate to Publishing Conditions section
4. Click Add condition
5. Select condition type "Store" and choose a specific store from the dropdown
6. Save the publishing
7. Verify the condition is saved
8. Open storefront for the specified store and verify content is visible
9. Open storefront for a different store and verify content is NOT visible

Title: Publishing Condition — Specific Contact
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Contact exists in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Navigate to Publishing Conditions section
4. Click Add condition
5. Select condition type targeting a specific contact or user
6. Search for and select an existing contact
7. Save the publishing
8. Verify the condition is saved with the contact name
9. Login to storefront as that contact and verify content visibility

Title: Content Scheduling — Start Date
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Click Edit to open the editing form
4. Set Start Date to a future date and time (e.g., tomorrow at 09:00)
5. Leave End Date empty
6. Save the publishing
7. Verify start date is saved and displayed correctly
8. Verify content is NOT visible on storefront before the start date
9. Advance system time or wait for start date and verify content becomes visible

Title: Content Scheduling — End Date
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing with Is Active = TRUE exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an active content publishing
3. Click Edit
4. Set End Date to a past date and time
5. Save the publishing
6. Verify the content publishing is now inactive or expired
7. Check storefront to confirm content is no longer visible
8. Set End Date to a future date and save
9. Verify content becomes visible on storefront again

Title: Content Scheduling — Date Range
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Click Edit
4. Set Start Date to a past date (e.g., yesterday)
5. Set End Date to a future date (e.g., next week)
6. Save the publishing
7. Verify content is currently visible on storefront (within the date range)
8. Update End Date to a past date and save
9. Verify content is no longer visible on storefront
10. Verify that Start Date before End Date validation is enforced

Title: Dynamic Expression Content Items — Create Expression-Based Content
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Dynamic expression content item type available
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Click on Content Items in the Dynamic Content section
3. Click Add to create a new content item
4. Select the expression-based or dynamic content type
5. Configure the expression with dynamic variables (e.g., customer name, product)
6. Enter the HTML template using expression syntax
7. Click Create and verify the item is created
8. Link the content item to a content publishing
9. Verify the expression is evaluated correctly on the storefront

Title: Search in Publishing List View — Use Search Bar to Find Published Content
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple content publishings exist with different names
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open the Content Publishing list
3. Click on the search bar
4. Enter a partial name of an existing content publishing
5. Verify the list filters to show matching results
6. Enter the full exact name and verify single result
7. Enter a name that does not match any publishing
8. Verify the list shows empty state or no results message
9. Clear the search field and verify the full list is restored

Title: Content item HTML XSS prevention
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Items 2. Click 'Add' to create a new content item 3. In the Name field enter: 'Test Item' 4. In the HTML/Content field enter: '

Test content

' 5. Click 'Create' and verify the item is saved 6. Open the created content item and verify the name is displayed safely (no script execution) 7. Link this content item to a content publishing with an active placeholder 8. Query the content via xAPI and inspect the returned HTML 9. Verify script tags are sanitized or escaped in the xAPI response 10. Check the storefront rendering to ensure no JavaScript executes from injected content

Title: Content item with special characters
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend
Steps:
1. Navigate to Marketing > Dynamic Content > Content Items
2. Create content item with Unicode name: 'Aktionstag Fruhling 2026'
3. Save and verify the name displays correctly with special characters
4. Create content item with emoji in name: 'Summer Sale'
5. Save and verify emoji renders correctly in the grid and blade
6. Create content item with very long name (255+ characters)
7. Verify validation: either truncates or shows max length error
8. Create content item with HTML entities in description: '& < > "'
9. Verify entities are stored and displayed correctly without double-encoding
10. Create content item with Cyrillic characters: 'Test content item name'
11. Verify multi-script text displays correctly
